exercises.name: Cable Leaning Step-Up
exercises.description: Lean slightly into the cable stack for a glute stretch at the bottom. Step up through your heel, squeezing at the top.

exercises.name: GHR — Rounded Back, Hip Thrust at Top
exercises.description: Glute-ham raise with a rounded back. At the top of each rep, thrust your hips forward for a full glute contraction.

exercises.name: Hip Abductor Machine
exercises.description: Sit upright, push knees apart against the pads. Squeeze at the end range, control the return.

exercises.name: Ab Wheel Rollout
exercises.description: From kneeling, roll out as far as you can control. Keep your core braced and hips extended — don't let your back sag.

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
exercises.description: Back against a bench, barbell across your hips. Drive through heels, squeeze glutes hard at the top. Go heavy.

exercises.name: Reverse Hyper
exercises.description: Lie face-down on the pad, swing legs up using glutes and hamstrings. Control the descent.

exercises.name: Cable Kickback
exercises.description: Ankle strap on the low cable. Drive your heel straight back, squeezing your glute at the top. Don't arch your back.

exercises.name: Kneeling Cable Crunch
exercises.description: Kneel in front of a high cable. Crunch down by flexing your abs, pulling your elbows toward your knees. Don't pull with your arms.

exercises.name: Deficit Reverse Lunge
exercises.description: Stand on a 25-lb plate. Step back into a reverse lunge, sinking deep for a glute stretch. Drive up through the front heel.

exercises.name: Frog Thrust
exercises.description: Feet together in a frog position, thrust your hips up squeezing your glutes at the top.

exercises.name: Hip Abductor Machine + Banded Lateral Walks
exercises.description: Superset: hip abductor machine for 20 reps, then immediately do banded lateral walks 20 steps each direction.

exercises.name: Weighted Decline Sit-Up
exercises.description: On a decline bench, hold a weight plate at your chest. Crunch up under control and lower slowly — don't let your back slam down.
